docs.title: Lunar Tear Private Server Setup Guide
Introduction
docs.text:
This guide will teach you how to setup the Lunar Tear private server. It's very noob friendly, so everyone should be able to follow. This setup is made for windows and for the android version of the game.

The official discord: discord.gg/MZAf5aVkJG

This guide wouldn't be possible without the amazing work of @Kretts and the dev team. Also a big thanks to @_REUSS for the russian guide. And all the people on discord, helping each other out.

@_REUSS russian guide: Google Docs

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
Tip

Always check the official repositories for the latest up to date files.

Lunar Tear repo Github

Lunar Tear repo Gitlab

Lunar Scripts repo Gitlab

docs.title: Guides
docs.text:
1 - Server Setup Guide

2 - Static IP

3 - Port Forwarding

4 - Public IP, Server Settings and new APK

Go Troubleshooting

docs.title: Adding Go to Path
docs.text: This page will teach you how to add Go to Path. It follows the same steps as adding make and protoc in the guide section.

docs.title: 1 - Locate the Go installation folder
docs.text:
Navigate to the folder Go\bin and copy it's location.

Note

If you installed Go with default settings the path will be C:\Program Files\Go\bin. If you installed it in another place copy that location.

docs.title: 2 - Open the System Settings
docs.text:
Open the Settings App. Select System>About and Advanced System Settings.

Press the Environment Variables button. Next double click on Path.

Press the New button and paste the location from above.

Press the Ok button in the windows to apply the settings.

docs.title: 3 - Checking if the Command Works
docs.text:
Note

If you have theCommand Prompt open, for any reason, close it and re-open it or the commands won't work.

Open the Command Prompt, type go and press enter.

Success

The command should work now, like in the image above.

docs.title: Static IP Guide
docs.text:
In order to create a server that others can connect to, you need a Static IP address. Without a Static IP, the IP address can change on its own, which would require you to change the server IP and patch the APK again.

This section of the guide will teach you how to change to a Static IP address.

Warning

The "Advanced Users" portion of the guide requires access to your home router. If you have never done this before, it is better to use a tool like Tailscale. Otherwise, you might mess up your internet settings. This warning will appear again before the section.

docs.title: 1 - Static IP via Windows Network Settings (Beginner Friendly)
docs.text:
The easiest way to set a static IP is in the Windows Network Settings. This avoids messing with your internet router and it's easy to fix if something doesn't work.

Info

This doesn't always work. Some ISPs only allow you to create a static IP address from within the router software. See the next section of the guide to learn how to do this.

docs.title: 1.1 - Open the Command Prompt
docs.text: Open the Command Prompt by typing cmd in Windows Search.

docs.title: 1.2 - Getting your router LAN IP and your computer IP
docs.text:
Type ipconfig in the Command Prompt and press Enter.

Since I'm using a Wi-Fi connection, my IP address appears under Wireless LAN adapter Wi-Fi. If you are using a cable connection, it will appear under Ethernet adapter.

Note

Your IP address will be different from the one shown in the images. Use the IP address that appears in your Command Prompt.

Copy the IPv4 Address, Default Gateway and Subnet Mask IPs and paste them in notepad.

IPv4 Address: xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx        #Computer IP
Default Gateway: xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx     #Router LAN IP
Subnet Mask: xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx

docs.title: 1.3 - Changing your network settings
docs.text:
Open the Control Panel and select Network and Internet.

Select Network and Sharing Center.

Click on your Internet Connection.

Next click on Properties and double click the IPv4 protocol.

Copy and paste the respective IPs and press Ok.

If you get this message press Yes.

Note

As mentioned in the beginning, this might not work. If your internet connection stops working after you change these settings, simply revert them to the automatic settings.

Warning

The "Advanced Users" portion of the guide requires access to your home router. If you have never done this before, it is better to use a tool like Tailscale. Otherwise, you might mess up your internet settings.

docs.title: 2 - Static IP via Router Settings (Advanced Users)
docs.text:
The process of changing to a Static IP via router varies between ISPs and router manufacturers. I will use my old home router (an Asus RT-AC51U) as an example. This will give you a rough idea on how to do it.

Tip

If you mess up your router settings, don't worry. They all have a reset button, which is a tiny hole that you can press with a toothpick. Pressing it will restore the default settings.

And rememeber to check your router manufacturer website. Some have detailed guides on how to do this process.

docs.title: 2.1 - Log in to your Router.
docs.text:
Copy the Default Gateway IP adress you got earlier in the guide and paste it on your web browser.

Note

Your IP address will be different from the one shown in the images. Use the IP address that appears in your Command Prompt.

The login page should load. Enter your username and password. Many routers have "admin" as the default username and password. Try that to see if it works. If not, search for your router model online.

username: admin
password: admin

docs.title: 2.2 - LAN settings
docs.text:
Select LAN in the Advanced Settings.

Select the DHCP tab and look for the higlited section.

docs.title: 2.3 - MAC Address and Static IP
docs.text:
Danger

Be careful about sharing your MAC address. It can compromise your security. Be safe.

Select your computer from the drop down menu. My computer is called "Gongaga".

If you don't know your device name you can input your MAC address. To get your address open the Command Prompt and copy and paste the following:

ipconfig /all

Look for Physical Address. Copy and paste it in the "Client Name (MAC Address)".

Next copy and paste your IP adress. Afterwards press the "+" button to add.

You will see your computer with the assigned IP adress. Press apply and wait.

Success

Now that you have a Static IP address, you can move on to the next step: Port Forwarding. Refer to the next section of the guide to continue.

Port Forwarding

docs.title: Public IP, Server Settings and new APK
docs.text:
We now only need to get our Public IP, update our server settings and build a new APK.

Danger

Be careful about sharing your Public IP. It can compromise your security. Be safe.

docs.title: 1 - Getting your Public IP
docs.text:
Open the website What Is My IP Address.

And copy the IPv4 address.

docs.title: 2.1 - Open the nier folder and type cmd to open the Command Prompt
2.2 - Run the Wizard and Update your IP
docs.text:
Copy and paste the following commands: 

cd server
go run ./cmd/wizard

A prompt will appear asking if you want to use the same settings as before, select "No, reconfigure".

Select "Phone / Tablet on the same network" and press Enter.

Next select "Something else / I'll type the IP" and press Enter.

Paste your Public IP and press Enter.

Finally chose "Yes, start".

Success

Your server is now running with your Public IP!

docs.title: 3 - Patching the APK
docs.text:
Note

This is an abridged version of the main setup guide. If you encounter any difficulties, please refer to Step 5 of the Server Setup Guide.

docs.title: 3.1 - Open the Website Google Colab and open lunar_tear_patcher.ipynb
3.2 - File Source
docs.text:
Copy and paste the command in the apk_source.

MyDrive/NieR Re[in]carnation 3.7.1.apk

docs.title: 3.3 - Server IPs
docs.text:
Note

Remember to use your own IPs. Not the ones in the images.

grpc_addr = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:8003"
http_addr = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:8080"
auth_host = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3000"

Copy and paste your Public IP in each field.

docs.title: 3.4 - Run the Patches
docs.text:
Press the Run button next to "Configuration".

Allow access to your Google Drive and let the patch run.

Next, press the Run button next to "Install dependencies" and let the patch run.

Do the same in the "Patch APK" section and let it run.

When the patching ends a new folder will appear in your drive named lunar-tear-output. Open the folder and download your new APK.

Success

You now have the new APK ready to be installed and to connect to your server!

docs.title: 4 - Enjoy playing with your friends in your server
docs.text:
Success

Everything is ready now! Have fun playing with your friends!

Tip

Should you ever need to change your server IP again, simply repeat the above steps.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
The prerequisites include both tools/programs and the server files.

Name Link lunar-tear-main.zip Github server repo lunar_tear_patcher.ipynb Gitlab scripts repo resource_dump_android.7z Archive.org torrent file NieR Re[in]carnation 3.7.1.apk Pixel Drain or Torrent 20240404193219.bin.e Mega Link Go 1.25+ go.dev Protoc Github protoc repo Make Make

Tip

To extract .zip and .7z I recommend Winrar or 7zip. To download the torrent file I recommend qBittorrent.

Warning

If you downloaded the APK from the APK Pure link, re-download it with the new links.

docs.title: Folder Structure
docs.text:
The folder structure doesn't include most files since they come together with the .zip files. Everything will be explained together with pictures.

nier/                     # The root folder of the guide.               
    server/               # Server repo (https://github.com/Walter-Sparrow/lunar-tear)
        assets/
            release/
            revisions/
        cmd/
        internal/
        migrations/
        proto/
20240404193219.bin.e
NieR Re[in]carnation 3.7.1.apk
lunar_tear_patcher.ipynb  # Scripts repo (https://gitlab.com/walter-sparrow-group/lunar-scripts)
resource_dump_android.7z

docs.title: 1.7 - Download protoc-35.0-rc-1-win64.zip from the Github Protoc Repo.
2.1 - Go
docs.text:
2.1.1 - Open go1.26.2.windows-amd64.msi and install it by pressing Next. Go should automatically add itself to Path.

Note

If the go command doesn't work after installing. Check this page.

docs.title: 2.2 - Protoc
docs.text:
2.2.1 - Extract protoc-35.0-rc-1-win64.zip

Tip

Extract the files to a safe location, so you don't delete them accidentally later.

2.2.2 - Navigate to protoc-35.0-rc-1-win64\bin and copy the location.

2.2.3 - Open the Settings App. Select System>About and Advanced System Settings.

2.2.4 - Press the Environment Variables button. Next double click on Path.

2.2.5 - Press the New button and paste the location from 2.2.2. Don't close the window yet.

docs.title: 2.3 - Make
docs.text:
2.3.1 - Open make-3.81.exe and install it by pressing Next.

2.3.2 - Navigate to C:\Program Files (x86)\GnuWin32\bin and copy the location.

2.3.3 - Go back to the window we oppened on 2.2.5 and do the same with the location.

You can now press Ok in the various windows to apply the Path.

docs.title: 2.4 - Checking if everything worked
docs.text:
Note

If you have theCommand Prompt open, for any reason, close it and re-open it or the commands won't work.

2.4.1 - Open the Command Prompt, type go and press enter.

2.4.2 - Next type protoc and press enter.

2.4.3 - Lastly type make and press enter.

If all the 3 commands showed up like in the images above, everything is good to go.

docs.title: 3.1 - Create a folder named nier
docs.text:
Note

After you create the nier folder, move 20240404193219.bin.e, resource_dump_android.7z, NieR Re[in]carnation 3.7.1.apk and lunar_tear_patcher.ipynb to it.

docs.title: 3.2 - Open the Command Prompt in the nier folder. To do this type cmd like in the image. Don't close the Command Prompt.
3.3 - Copy the commands bellow, one by one, to create the necessary folders. Or you can do it manually.
docs.text:
mkdir "server/assets/release"
mkdir "server/assets/revisions"

docs.title: 3.4 - Open the lunar-tear-main.zip and extract the server folder to the nier folder.
3.5 - Go to the nier\server\assets\revisions folder. After that open resource_dump_android.7z\revisions and extract the folder 0. This will take some time.
docs.text:
Tip

Everything should be ready to go. If for some reason something fails, check the folder structure and give the guide another look.

docs.title: 4.1 - Go back to the Command Prompt in the nier folder we oppened on 3.2 and enter the following commands one at the time.
docs.text:
cd server
go run ./cmd/wizard --setup-only

4.1.1 - Android Emulator

Select the android emulator you are using. If you don't know choose the Other / Not Sure option.

4.1.2 - Phone / Tablet

Chose whatever option you intend to use.

Note

If you choose the first option Same Wi-Fi Network, remember to use a static IP adress or the IP could change between sessions.

docs.title: 4.2 - After choosing the option, the program will auto detect your IP adress. Press enter.
docs.text:
Note

Your IP address will be different from the one shown in the images. Use the IP address that appears in your Command Prompt.

grpc_addr = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:8003"
http_addr = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:8080"
auth_host = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3000"

You can either copy and paste directly from the Command Prompt or save the IP in a Notebook file on your computer.

docs.title: Step 5 - Patching with Google Colab
docs.text:
Note

You must have a Google account to use Google Drive and Google Colab.

docs.title: 5.1 - Upload 20240404193219.bin.e and NieR Re[in]carnation 3.7.1.apk to the root folder of your Google Drive.
5.2 - Open the Website Google Colab and Open lunar_tear_patcher.ipynb.
docs.text: If you are already logged in, this window should pop-up. If not use the next image.

docs.title: 5.3 - File Sources
docs.text:
Copy and paste the first command in the apk_source and the second command in the masterdata_source.

MyDrive/NieR Re[in]carnation 3.7.1.apk
MyDrive/20240404193219.bin.e

docs.title: 5.4 - Server IPs
docs.text:
Note

Remember to use your own IPs. Not the ones in the images.

grpc_addr = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:8003"
http_addr = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:8080"
auth_host = "xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3000"

Copy each IP to the corresponding field.

docs.title: 5.5 - Run the Patch
docs.text:
Press the Run all button.

Allow access to your Google Drive and let the patch run.

When the patching ends a new folder will appear in your drive named lunar-tear-output. Open the folder and download both files.

docs.title: 5.6 - The new 20240404193219.bin.e
docs.text: Copy the new 20240404193219.bin.e to nier\server\assets\release.

docs.title: 6.1 - Open the Command Prompt in the nier folder again and run the commands below.
docs.text:
cd server
go run ./cmd/wizard

The wizard will download and install dependencies.

When it ends, a prompt will appear. Select Yes.

After that 3 windows will appear. Allow access to all of them.

docs.title: 6.2 - The Server is now working!
docs.text:
Tip

You can create a .bat file in the nier folder to run the server. Just copy the commands in 6.1 to a text file and save as Run_Server.bat

docs.title: 7.1 - Open your android emulator or grab your phone/tablet. And drag and drop patched.apk
7.2 - Open the game and Tap Start!
docs.text: If this screen appears, congratulations, everything is working and you can start playing!

docs.title: 7.3 - Creating and Linking your account
docs.text:
7.3.1 - Select the Menu option.

7.3.2 - Select the Transfer Data option.

7.3.3 - Select the Facebook sign in.

7.3.4 - Choose an username and a password to create your account.

docs.title: 7.4 - Linking account to a new device
docs.text:
Note

Before you can log in to your account you must clear app data in the android settings.

7.4.1 - On the tittle screen press the button in the upper-right corner.

7.4.2 - Choose the Transfer Data option.

7.4.3 - Press the Facebook button.

7.4.4 - And sign in with your account.

Note

Everytime you want to switch device you must transfer data. The game doesn't allow multiple devices.

Success

Your server is now fully functional, so you can start enjoying the game! Have fun!

docs.title: 8 - Next Steps (Optional)
docs.text:
Now that you have a server up and running, you may want to play with friends or other people. To do so, you will need to set up a Static IP address and Port Forwarding.

Check the Static IP page to learn what to do next.

docs.title: Port Forwarding Guide (Advanced Users)
docs.text:
Now that you have set up your Static IP, you will need to open the ports. This is used to let a user connect from their computer to your server.

Warning

This portion of the guide requires access to your home router. If you have never done this before, it is better to use a tool like Tailscale. Otherwise, you might mess up your internet settings.

docs.title: 1 - Port Forwarding
docs.text:
The process of enabling Port Forwarding via router varies between ISPs and router manufacturers. I will use my old home router (an Asus RT-AC51U) as an example. This will give you a rough idea on how to do it.

Tip

If you mess up your router settings, don't worry. They all have a reset button, which is a tiny hole that you can press with a toothpick. Pressing it will restore the default settings.

And rememeber to check your router manufacturer website. Some have detailed guides on how to do this process.

docs.title: 1.1 - Login to your Router.
docs.text:
Copy the Default Gateway IP adress you got earlier in the Static IP Guide and paste it on your web browser.

Note

Your IP address will be different from the one shown in the images. Use the IP address that appears in your Command Prompt.

The login page should load. Enter your username and password. Many routers have "admin" as the default username and password. Try that to see if it works. If not, search for your router model online.

username: admin
password: admin

docs.title: 1.2 - WAN Settings
docs.text:
Select WAN in the Advanced Settings. Go to the "Virtual Server / Port Forwarding" and enable Port Forwarding.

Scroll until you see the "Port Forwarding List" section.

docs.title: 1.3 - Opening Ports
docs.text:
In the 'Service Name' field, enter the name you want. I chose 'Nier Server'. In the 'Port Range' field we need to enter the ports the server uses.

Note

These ports are the same ones we used during the main setup guide.

Copy and paste the ports from bellow.

8003,8080,3000

Enter the static IP address that we set up in the 'Static IP' section of the guide into the 'Local IP' field.

Press the "+" button to add the new settings and "Apply" button to save changes.

Success

You now have a Static IP address and have enabled Port Forwarding! The final step is to obtain your public IP address, adjust the server settings and create the new APK.
